_comment: Special-element annotation overlay for Book II Episode 2 (the Lessons chapter). Covers all non-standard printed elements per the Hart Concordance: sigla, drawings, music, math, and Greek. Source: Hart Concordance complete special-elements pages; McHugh (1976) p.133.
source: Hart Concordance complete special-elements pages; McHugh (1976) p.133.
encoding_note: See data/annotations/README.md for the full sigla Unicode reference table.

Ⅎ.note: The 'backwards F' (horizontally mirrored) has no standard Unicode equivalent. U+2132 TURNED CAPITAL F (rotated 180°) is the closest available. McHugh (1976) p.145: 'the laterally inverted pairs of Fs to be encountered at 018.36, 121.03, 07 and 266.22 — their mystery remains closed'. They are distinct from the character-sigla and do not appear in the 299.F4 family listing.

footnote_reconstruction.scraped_text: 188 .    The Doodles family, Hoodle doodle, fam.?
footnote_reconstruction.strip_note: Trent (like telelib) strips all 7 sigla from the footnote text.

sequence_from_1939_edition.note: HCE (Humphrey Chimpden Earwicker). Confirmed by McHugh p.144: 'Why not call M Earwicker'. Visually resembles a blocky M/Ш-like character. Distinct from Ǝ (U+018E), HCE's 'E pointing down' form at I.5 119.17 — HCE has multiple printed forms.
ALP (Anna Livia Plurabelle). Clear triangle shape. McHugh's A is an ASCII approximation of the triangle.

Issy (Isabel). Concordance position 3 = Issy. McHugh approximates this as '4'. Not the same character as I.2 036.17 — McHugh also calls that character '4' but it is a distinct siglum used by HCE to address the cad; the two share only McHugh's imprecise ASCII approximation.
The Four (four old men / four evangelists: Matthew, Mark, Luke, John). Concordance position 4 = Four. A fourfold cross shape. NOT Shem — that was an error in earlier annotation.

House (the Earwicker inn / tavern). Concordance position 5 = House. McHugh p.143: 'O as their old fourwheedler which might stand for the bucker's field' — the square represents the building. NOT Shaun the person — that was an error.

Shaun the Post. Concordance position 6 = Shaun. Wedge/caret shape. McHugh uses A a second time (same ASCII letter as position 2 for ALP — his approximations are not unique). NOT Issy — that was an error.

Shem the Penman. Concordance position 7 = Shem. McHugh p.143: 'C here is the family gibbet' — the C/hook-shaped character is Shem's siglum. McHugh uses £ in 299.F4 and C when describing the I.5 passage — both approximate the same reversed-bracket/hook shape.

footnote_reconstruction.note: Sequence confirmed from 1939 Faber & Faber edition scan (page 299, footnote 4). Referents confirmed from Hart Concordance Symbols page. All Unicode approximations are uncertain — the 1939 characters are hand-drawn with no standard Unicode equivalent. This is the most complete printed listing of the full siglum cast in FW.

drawings.visual_description: Two overlapping circles forming a Vesica Piscis. Labeled: π at the top intersection, P at the bottom intersection, Aα inside the left circle, λL inside the right circle. A triangle (mandorla/lens shape) is inscribed at the intersection, with dashed lines indicating the inverted triangle below.
drawings.note: Concordance labels this 'view from Dublin' from the surrounding text ('Vieus Von DVbLIn', section 102), but the drawing itself is a geometric Vesica Piscis diagram. Labels: π and P are intersection points (P = the bishop siglum, per McHugh p.142-143); Aα and λL label the two circles. McHugh devotes a full chapter to the Vesica Piscis in FW. Completely absent from the Trent corpus.

drawings.visual_description: A hand making a nose-thumbing gesture: thumb extended toward the nose, four fingers spread and raised. Drawn in outline style.
drawings.note: Footnote drawing on the last page of II.2. Completely absent from the Trent corpus.

drawings.visual_description: Two spoons crossed at their necks in an X pattern, with ornate round bowl-ends at the bottom and decorative handles (with small fork-like tines) at the top. Drawn in outline style.
drawings.note: Footnote drawing on the last page of II.2. Completely absent from the Trent corpus.

music.note: Musical note names in the left-hand margin at page 272 line 9. Completely absent from the Trent corpus left_margin field — stripped with no trace.

math.note: CONFIRMED IN CORPUS. ∞ survives in II.2 main text at line 284.11. The concordance listing confirms this is intentional (Joyce drawing on Bertrand Russell's Introduction to Mathematical Philosophy, 1919).
CONFIRMED IN CORPUS. ∴ survives in II.2 main text at line 292.11.
CONFIRMED IN CORPUS. ∵ survives in II.2 main text at line 292.12.

greek.note: CONFIRMED IN CORPUS. Greek text 'ουκ ελβον πολιν' survives in II.2 left_margin field for line 269.24.

annotations.note: Concordance 266.22: 'F, backwards F'. A plain F and a backwards-F appear as a pair. The plain F likely survives in the corpus; the backwards-F was stripped. 1939 context: 'wranglers for wringwrowdy wready are, F [Ⅎ], (at gaze, respecting'. char_offset is -1 pending verification against Trent corpus line 266.22.
